feature.title: Global Dispatch Console
feature.purpose: Give the Agent Company Atlas a centralized local command outbox where bot instructions can be staged, copied, archived, and traced back to source lanes without sending messages or starting workers.

research.name: Command Palette
research.takeaway: Command surfaces should help operators find and run actions from one reliable place while preserving state, rules, and recovery paths.

research.name: Designing Command Palettes
research.takeaway: Command palettes are action surfaces, not just search surfaces, and are most useful for power users in complex software.

research.name: Human-in-the-Loop AI Agents
research.takeaway: Agent systems should keep human approval and oversight visible before risky or externally side-effecting work.

research.name: Game Dispatch UI Reference
research.takeaway: Dispatch interfaces can make mission assignment feel tangible through cards, status, and staged actions.

implementation.safetyBoundary: Local staging and copying only. The feature does not send messages, mutate the control plane, start workers, or approve gates.
interactions: Stage a suggested lane command into a browser-local outbox.
Persist staged drafts across page reloads.
Archive staged drafts from the local queue.
Open the source lane Comms deck from a suggestion or staged command.

snapshot.topSuggestion: Review gate before any live action
